adoption_plans.executive_summary: Waterloo Public Library shows strong potential for AI-assisted workflow improvement, particularly in follow-up coordination and program documentation. A structured adoption approach starting with a limited pilot and strong human-review processes is recommended. Staff concerns about workload and evaluation have been noted and should be addressed proactively.
current_workflow_context: Manual follow-up email drafting for workshop participants is time-consuming
Program documentation is inconsistent across different staff members
Meeting notes are not consistently captured or shared

opportunities.name: AI-Assisted Program Documentation
opportunities.pain_point: Time-consuming writing and formatting, inconsistent quality
opportunities.possible_ai_support: Generate first drafts from bullet points, notes, or outlines

opportunities.name: Automated Meeting Notes & Action Items
opportunities.pain_point: Inconsistent notes, action items get lost
opportunities.possible_ai_support: Convert rough notes into structured summaries with action items
opportunities.expected_benefit: Consistent follow-up and fewer missed items

recommended_first_pilot.problem_statement: Staff spend significant time drafting follow-up emails after workshops and programs, leading to delayed or inconsistent communication.
recommended_first_pilot.scope_limit: Limited to follow-up emails for one program area with 2-3 participating staff.
success_metrics: Time spent drafting follow-ups per week
Number of edits required per AI draft
stop_or_revise_criteria: AI output quality is consistently poor

roadmap_steps.stage: Validate follow-up pain point with staff
Run pilot with one program area

training_plan.description: How AI assistance works and limitations
Writing effective prompts for email drafting
Evaluating AI output for email content

training_recommendations.note: Training should be tied to the actual pilot workflow, not delivered as generic tool instruction.

items.question: Is there a safe feedback loop?

risks_and_mitigation.risk: Staff may feel AI adds to workload
risks_and_mitigation.mitigation: Set clear expectations that AI is assistive

recommended_next_meeting_questions: Which follow-up tasks take the most staff time each week?
What would make you feel confident in AI-generated email drafts?

adoption_plans.executive_summary: Kitchener Community Food Centre shows potential for AI-assisted workflow improvement, particularly in documentation and follow-up tasks. A structured adoption approach starting with a limited pilot and strong human-review processes is recommended.
current_workflow_context: Donation intake tracking requires repetitive data entry
Client communication is handled ad-hoc without templates
Grant reporting requires manual data compilation from multiple spreadsheets

opportunities.name: Volunteer Schedule Matching and Communication
opportunities.current_workflow: Volunteer scheduling managed through shared spreadsheet. Coordinator spends 4-6 hours per week on schedule matching and email confirmations.
opportunities.pain_point: Manual schedule matching and individual confirmation emails for 40+ active volunteers
opportunities.possible_ai_support: AI-assisted schedule matching based on availability, skills, and program needs with automated confirmation draft generation
required_human_review: Schedule changes and volunteer preference exceptions require human handling
risks_or_exceptions: Some volunteers may struggle with technology changes

opportunities.name: Donation Intake Logging and Receipt Generation
opportunities.current_workflow: Donations logged manually into a tracking spreadsheet. Staff spend 8-10 hours per week on data entry and receipt generation.
opportunities.pain_point: Time-consuming manual data entry for donation tracking and tax receipt lookups
opportunities.possible_ai_support: Structured donation intake form with automated logging and receipt generation, OCR for handwritten donation notes
required_human_review: Food quality notes and special handling requirements need human review
risks_or_exceptions: OCR may inaccurately capture handwritten donation details
Food quality context may be lost in structured fields

opportunities.name: Multi-Funder Grant Report Compilation
opportunities.current_workflow: Data extracted manually from donation logs, volunteer hours, and attendance records for each funder's specific format. 6-8 hours per quarter per grant.
opportunities.pain_point: Each funder requires different metrics and formats, requiring manual data extraction from multiple sources
opportunities.possible_ai_support: Standardized data collection with AI-assisted report drafting that adapts content to each funder's required format and metrics
required_human_review: Program impact context requires human narrative input that structured data may not capture
risks_or_exceptions: AI may miss important program impact context not in structured data

opportunities.name: Automated Meeting Notes & Action Items
opportunities.current_workflow: Staff take notes during meetings, then manually extract action items
opportunities.pain_point: Notes are inconsistent; action items get lost
opportunities.possible_ai_support: Convert rough notes into structured summaries with action items
opportunities.expected_benefit: Consistent follow-up and fewer missed items

recommended_first_pilot.problem_statement: Manual schedule matching and individual confirmation emails for 40+ active volunteers
recommended_first_pilot.current_process: Volunteer scheduling managed through shared spreadsheet. Coordinator spends 4-6 hours per week on schedule matching and email confirmations.
recommended_first_pilot.proposed_ai_assisted_process: AI-assisted schedule matching based on availability, skills, and program needs with automated confirmation draft generation
recommended_first_pilot.scope_limit: Limited to one workflow area and 2-3 participating staff members. No external-facing AI-generated content without human approval.
required_inputs: Sample documents or notes from the selected workflow
Access to knowledge sources used in the workflow
human_review_checkpoints: Before AI output is shared externally
Weekly review of AI output quality by coordinator
privacy_or_quality_risks: AI may generate plausible-sounding but incorrect information
Staff may over-rely on AI output without critical review
Privacy if client data is used in prompts
success_metrics: Time spent on the workflow task (before vs. during pilot)
Number of AI outputs requiring significant revision
Staff confidence rating (survey before and after)
Number of follow-ups completed on time
stop_or_revise_criteria: Staff report increased workload instead of reduction
AI output quality is consistently poor despite adjustments
Privacy concerns emerge that cannot be addressed
Staff feedback indicates loss of confidence or trust

adoption_plans.human_review_model: All AI outputs require human review before external use. Designate a staff reviewer for each workflow area.

items.question: Have staff concerns been recorded and addressed?
items.notes: Review adoption risk notes for staff concerns

items.question: Does the pilot reduce workload rather than simply increase output expectations?
Are human-review responsibilities clearly defined?
items.notes: Document who reviews what and when

items.question: Are staff expected to share useful workflows or prompts?
Is there a plan to explain how performance will be evaluated during the pilot?
items.notes: Pilot is for learning, not evaluation

items.question: Are employees protected from being penalized for using AI responsibly?
Are failure cases and exceptions documented?
Is there a safe feedback loop during the pilot?
Is there a decision process for expanding or stopping the pilot?

training_plan.description: How AI assistance works, its limitations, and responsible use boundaries

training_plan.topic: Prompt Crafting for the Selected Workflow
training_plan.description: How to write effective prompts for Volunteer Schedule Matching and Communication
training_plan.recommended_format: Hands-on session with real workflow examples

training_plan.description: How to critically evaluate AI output, what to check, and when to override
What data can be used in AI prompts, privacy boundaries, and confidentiality requirements

additional_topics.description: Documenting new AI-assisted workflows for team sharing
How to identify and document cases where AI output is incorrect or insufficient

training_plan.note: Training should be tied to the actual pilot workflow, not delivered as generic tool instruction. Focus on practical application.

metrics.name: Time spent on workflow task
metrics.baseline: Measure current time before pilot starts

metrics.name: Output quality / revision rate
metrics.baseline: All outputs are currently manual (100% human effort)
metrics.target: Less than 30% of AI outputs require major revision
metrics.measurement_method: Track revision rate of AI-assisted outputs

metrics.name: Reusable workflow documentation
metrics.target: At least 1 documented workflow shared with the team
metrics.measurement_method: Count of documented and shared workflows

risks_and_mitigation.risk: Staff worry AI-generated communications may feel impersonal for clients in vulnerable situations
risks_and_mitigation.mitigation: Develop flexible templates with tone options and require staff personalization review before sending to clients

risks_and_mitigation.risk: Staff evaluation criteria do not account for improved reporting efficiency, only program outcomes
risks_and_mitigation.mitigation: Update evaluation criteria to recognize administrative improvements and efficiency gains from AI-assisted workflows

recommended_next_meeting_questions: Which workflow would your team most like to test with AI assistance?
What would make you feel confident in AI-assisted output quality?
What concerns do you have about how AI might affect your team's workflow?

pilot_plans.problem_statement: Staff at Waterloo Public Library spend significant time manually drafting follow-up emails after workshops.
pilot_plans.current_process: Staff manually compose individual follow-up emails, searching for past correspondence and program details.
pilot_plans.proposed_ai_assisted_process: AI generates first-draft follow-up emails from program notes. Staff review, edit, and approve before sending.
pilot_plans.scope_limit: Limited to follow-up emails for one program area with 2-3 participating staff.
human_review_checkpoints: Weekly review of AI output quality
